issues_found: event_type 'monetary_policy_report' is borderline; this is a press release announcing framework approval, not a full report—the more precise type would be 'fomc_statement' or similar
Source is inherently thin—announces the update but does not specify what changes were made to the framework statement
The actual framework changes are in a linked PDF not included in this source, limiting archival value for substantive content
No specific policy signals or forward guidance language provided in this source
human_review_reason: The record is factually accurate and source attribution is correct, but the event_type may be imprecise and the source is thin with no substantive content on actual framework changes. Human review needed to assess if 'monetary_policy_report' is the correct event_type or if this should be reclassified as something like 'fomc_statement'. Also note the source was published 221 days before TODAY (well past 90-day threshold)—acceptable as historical policy document but worth flagging.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: The why_it_matters correctly notes that 'the source does not specify what changes were made'—this is accurate and appropriately caveats the record.
